subject: Add m2 model, including the LCM application
commit_message:
Add m2 model, including the LCM application

Issue-ID: POLICY-1948
Change-Id: I18a5231d3102073c928a591c9e91b241b7093680
Signed-off-by: Straubs, Ralph (rs8887) <rs8887@att.com>

This was not what I expected at all.

Can you be more specific on this?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2
> > 
> > This was not what I expected at all.
> 
> Can you be more specific on this?

Just the guard and operations history itself is out-of-date and not compatible. What is the LCM code doing here? Looks like its specific to appc only. Now I've got 2 separate implementations of drools rules. One that sits in an "m2" submodule and the rest elsewhere. How does anyone sort this out?

Patch Set 1:

> Patch Set 1:
> 
> > Patch Set 1:
> > 
> > > Patch Set 1: Code-Review-2
> > > 
> > > This was not what I expected at all.
> > 
> > Can you be more specific on this?
> 
> Just the guard and operations history itself is out-of-date and not compatible. What is the LCM code doing here? Looks like its specific to appc only. Now I've got 2 separate implementations of drools rules. One that sits in an "m2" submodule and the rest elsewhere. How does anyone sort this out?

Hi Pam.  The plan was to contribute the m2 code and then move the ONAP use cases to using it once it's in ONAP.  This avoids a flash cut over.  Once done, any old code can be removed from ONAP.  Regarding the other questions we will setup a meeting to review with you.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(145 comments)

Besides the comments below, I have a couple of general comments:
- I briefly checked the code coverage on one of the sub-projects and it was only 40%.
  It may reflect a higher coverage once all tests are run, but it certainly leaves me
  doubtful whether it has high enough coverage
- Eclipse won't let me import maven projects having the same name as separate projects
  (e.g., "guard" under m2 clashes with "guard" under controlloop).  If I just import the
  top-level project, then the code linking to class definitions doesn't work.  Hopefully
  there's something I can configure to make this work
- remove unused imports

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

(27 comments)

I think in general there some areas that do not fit well with existing software.   I understand though that this is work in progress though and eventually the hope is that all gaps are bridged and we arrive to a design/implemetation that is agreed to be better by everybody.

I think it would be better to stage the work in multiple smaller reviews too, so there is more meaningful and more focused discussions.   A single one is a bit overwhelming.   I think smaller reviews would facilitate discussion, and we can come up with concrete plans of action to move forward on this work and for full integration.   Right now with this large piece of work is difficult to put the arms around it, and come up with concrete plans of action moving forward to bridge the gaps.

As an aside, one general comment is the location where this work should go, we had the controlloop module to do controlloop stuff, seems that the code should go there.   Along the same lines, it would be desirable to start with features that could be off in our system, specially for when we hit the labs, and it will also reflect that this area is in flux as some areas are very divergent for example guard, pdp-x communication with existing code.   Eventually if solid, they could be promoted to main base after being blessed by testing.

Eventually if solid, they could be promoted to main base after being blessed by testing.

Agreed - for this work it is more appropriate to do smaller or parallel upgrades in the codebase.

The first task to be done is getting the Actor interface correct with callbacks and ensuring the API's called by each of the actors is done consistently. The current actor code is a mish-mash of static calls vs instance methods. It has no thought regarding threading or multiple requests.

Patch Set 4:

Thanks for the work Ralph, here are some comments, my intent is that they are constructive:

The code is well documented, and there is the clear intent to performing operations asynchronously.  The resulting drl simplifies to a large degree what we have over our current base, and I can see how the approach will improve performance.  

To keep things in perspective, the final goal of this contribution is that it is usable in the ONAP use cases, such as the scale out, vFirewall, etc .. and that it significantly improves our code base.

On the pending work side, the review does not integrate just yet with the existing ONAP base.   I understand that the proposal is that this will be done later on after this submission, and from my side I would be OK with it.  The concern is that it seems to me there is quite a bit of work left.

In any case, these are the functional areas for what I see in this review that still need to be tackled:

1. Integration with usecases style drl with ONAP flows.  In this drl, there is not template expansion, no need to store loggers in contexts, etc.  

2. Simulators, need to reconcile existing and new provided implementations, determine which one is better, and use only 1 to avoid the code duplication.

3. Guard, I think there are still 2 versions of the code updating the DB.

4. I think similarly for the Appc actor, it is my impression that there is still some overlap with that in policy-models, although I am not entirely sure.

From my side, I would be OK staging the pending work after this one, as long as there is a firm commitment to get it accomplished.   This agreement could be documented in the form of high priority JIRA tickets and would need to be tackled right after this review in Frankfurt.

Another request is to encapsulate this work on its own feature, so:

5. Encapsulate all the work on an m2 feature, to make this functionality toggable and therefore optional.

In addition, since these projects are "control loop" related, they should be moved under the controlloop directory.

With regards to 5 and 6., similarly, I am OK with postponing it to right after this review.

There is also the sonar and junit work to keep up with the numbers currently have in this repo.

As an aside, something that worries me somewhat is the parallel work on the actor clean up implementation task that may be going on in parallel, that would also need to be synced at some point.

(1 comment)

Regarding integration with ONAP -- this is a new high-level framework (new to ONAP -- it is actually about 2 1/2 years old). It had the following design goals (taken from the m2 model viewgraph presentation):

1) Use a single object in Drools memory for each transaction

2) Make the state more explicit

3) Incoming events are transient

4) No actor-specific or operator-specific code within the transaction object

Much of the low-level code was reused, but the high-level control code in the older model was completely replaced. Everything was designed around the 'Transaction', 'Actor', and 'Operation' classes, and these were designed with performance and extensibility in mind.

Regarding integration with ONAP, the big question is whether there is general buy-in to this architecture. If people agree that this is the way to go, "integration with ONAP" involves adding other 'Actor' and 'Operation' subclasses to cover current ONAP functionality that does not yet exist in m2. If people don't agree, then this was a wasted effort, and it would be better to not merge this change.

Regarding item #1, the only '.drl' file we have currently provided is one used in the 'LcmTest' Junit test. We do have other ECOMP-specific files. All of them are currently based upon templates, but we do plan to move away from that.

Regarding item #2, I will try to find time to look at and try out existing simulators. However, the ONAP simulators seem to have an order of magnitude more code, which I don't regard as a good sign.

Regarding item #3, I did look at the existing code updating the DB, and it is in 'ControlLoopOperationManager', deep within the bowels of the existing model. I didn't see any good way to call this code, but I did make use of the 'Dbao' class. The query portion was easier to reuse, because it wasn't coupled to the existing model.

Regarding item #4, we are using ONAP lower-level code, but the higher-level control code needs to be compatible with the m2 control structure, so it is different. 'LcmResponseCode' might be the one class that could be shared -- I don't remember the details, but I probably looked at this earlier, and decided we needed a different class.

Regarding item #5, we did discuss this earlier, and I agree that we should move the code and make it a feature. I was hoping to do this after we resolved other issues, because moving code makes it more difficult to compare patch sets.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

Please next step would be to move this work onto its own feature under the controlloop package.  Needless to say sonar and coverage should be followed up to match current metrics / silver badge certification reqs.

Patch Set 6:

> Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1
> 
> Please next step would be to move this work onto its own feature under the controlloop package.  Needless to say sonar and coverage should be followed up to match current metrics / silver badge certification reqs.

I have moved the 'm2' directory under 'controlloop', and created a subproject 'feature-controlloop-m2' underneath. It has all of the other 'm2' subprojects as dependencies, except for 'test'. I preferred this approach to the following alternative approaches: 1) creating separate features for each 'm2' subproject and 2) merging all of the 'm2' subprojects into a single project, and making that the feature.

Build Failed 

https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/1246/ : FAILURE

Unable to build due to a non-resolvable parent POM. Search logs for "[FATAL] Non-resolvable parent POM" to find the logs.

The fix for this is usually to make sure your poms are pointing to parents that exist. Double check:

 * groupId is correct and no typos
 * artifactId is correct and no typos
 * version is correct for branch ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/1246/ )

There are test failures. Please look in the log to identify them. ( https://jenkins.onap.org/job/policy-drools-applications-master-verify-java/1246/ )
